headline: Prime Video's ad-free plan gets a price increase, and a new name
snippet: If you're one of the many paying for ad-free Prime Video, we have some bad news. Today, Amazon announced two big changes to the service. It's getting a large price increase to $5 per month, and moving
full_text: If you're one of the many paying for ad-free Prime Video, we have some bad news. Today, Amazon announced two big changes to the service. It's getting a large price increase to $5 per month, and moving forward, it'll be called Prime Video Ultra. Remember when Prime Video was free and included with your Amazon Prime membership? Technically, that's still true, but now it comes with ads. In October 2024, Amazon began showing even more ads and offered an ad-free experience for $3 per month. The new Prime Video Ultra is now $5 per month Pay a little more to remove ads In 2024, with the addition of ads, Amazon immediately received some pushback, since many customers feel they're already paying for it via Amazon Prime and that it shouldn't have ads. That didn't matter, and now to make things worse, those who want to enjoy all their favorite Prime Video shows and movies without ads will start paying $5 per month (up from $3) starting on April 10th, 2026.
